records.ety: [L. penetratus, p. p. of penetrare to penetrate; akin to penitus inward, inwardly, and perh. to pens with, in the power of, penus store of food, innermost part of a temple.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Penetrated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Penetrating.]

defs: To enter into; to make way into the interior of; to effect an entrance into; to pierce; as, light penetrates darkness.
To affect profoundly through the senses or feelings; to touch with feeling; to make sensible; to move deeply; as, to penetrate one's heart with pity.
quotes: The translator of Homer should penetrate himself with a sense of the plainness and directness of Homer's style.

defs: To pierce into by the mind; to arrive at the inner contents or meaning of, as of a mysterious or difficult subject; to comprehend; to understand.
quotes: Things which here were too subtile for us to penetrate.

defs: To pass; to make way; to pierce. Also used figuratively.
quotes: Preparing to penetrate to the north and west.
Born where Heaven's influence scarce can penetrate.
The sweet of life that penetrates so near.
